name: Non-LLM Text Augmentation for Programming ASAG
statement: Bonthu, Rama Sree, and Krishna Prasad report a programming-domain ASAG study in which text augmentation techniques supplemented limited short-answer training data for sentence-transformer scoring models.
population_or_context: Programming-related short answers in the SPRAG corpus.
time_horizon: 2023 publication; non-LLM text augmentation paired with sentence-transformer transfer learning.
quantitative_note: The official abstract reports a 4.91% accuracy increase and a 3.36% F1-score increase for random swap plus synonym replacement, but full tables were not extracted.
evaluation_method: Accuracy and F1 comparisons across augmentation conditions according to the official abstract.
sparsity_or_imbalance_motivation: The abstract frames ASAG performance limitations around limited data and lack of available data in many domains.
synthetic_data_research_role: Training-data supplementation for ASAG model development under limited domain-specific data.
reported_metrics: Accuracy and F1-score improvements reported in the official abstract; table-level results pending full-text access.
limitations: Extraction is limited to the official abstract/search-indexed ScienceDirect text.
Exact sample sizes, splits, and table-level metric values still need full-text verification.
source_locator: ScienceDirect abstract/highlights and Crossref metadata; direct full-text fetch blocked by Cloudflare on 2026-06-03.
